meta.title: BCO Content Skills Tracker
meta.description: Tracking how content design, content systems, and language infrastructure skills are evolving across industries.

entries.title: Content Engineer
entries.coreFrame: Language infrastructure, AI agents, UX writing, brand communication

entries.title: QA Engineer — Analytics Platform
entries.coreFrame: Quality assurance, technical writing, and enablement for an AI data agent

entries.title: Content Systems Architect
entries.coreFrame: AI-powered content production workflows for editorial series

entries.title: Senior Global UX Writer, Content Systems
entries.coreFrame: Taxonomy, terminology governance, localization architecture, content-as-code

entries.title: Content Architect
entries.coreFrame: Content modeling, structured data, migrations, governance

entries.title: Senior Content Designer, Language Systems
entries.coreFrame: Language governance, AI tool evaluation, design system integration

entries.title: Product Designer
entries.coreFrame: Product design with LLM-first workflow and AI prototyping as stated process steps

entries.title: Annotation Manager — Content Platform
entries.coreFrame: ML data labeling, annotation guidelines, content classification workflows

entries.title: Editor, AI Content Systems
entries.coreFrame: AI editorial QA, prompt workflows, SEO content production

entries.title: Content Designer
entries.coreFrame: Product language infrastructure, terminology systems, AI-generated copy standards, content frameworks

entries.title: Senior UX Content Designer, Search
entries.coreFrame: AI model behavior design, conversational response strategy, generative AI search UX

entries.title: Content Design, Vice President
entries.coreFrame: Modular content systems for multi-partner co-brand card portfolio, content architecture, governance, legal/compliance content standards

entries.title: Staff Content Designer, NCXD
entries.coreFrame: Language frameworks, terminology systems, multi-platform content design (TV, mobile, web), globalization partnership, 0-to-1 product initiatives

entries.title: Product Content Engineer
entries.coreFrame: GenAI content quality standards, editorial guidelines as reusable frameworks, annotation quality control for model training, measurement frameworks

entries.title: Senior UX Writer and Content Designer, Chrome
entries.coreFrame: Content design for AI Mode features in Chrome, prompt design, system instructions, writing guidelines and word lists, trust and safety content

terminology-governance.label: Terminology & Language Governance
terminology-governance.description: Defining canonical terms, controlled vocabularies, and a single source of truth for product language.

content-systems-design.label: Content Systems Design
content-systems-design.description: Frameworks, patterns, templates, and reusable structures for consistent content at scale.

ai-tooling.label: AI Tooling & Automation
ai-tooling.description: Building, evaluating, or operationalizing AI-powered content workflows.

ai-content-governance.label: AI-Generated Content Governance
ai-content-governance.description: Standards, quality rubrics, prompt templates, and tone constraints for AI-produced content.

taxonomy.label: Taxonomy & Information Architecture
taxonomy.description: Classification systems, metadata structures, and content models.

localization.label: Localization & Internationalization
localization.description: String structure, variable logic, culturally neutral source copy, and translation-ready frameworks.

enablement.label: Enablement & Team Upskilling
enablement.description: Playbooks, guidelines, and tools that help non-writers produce quality content.

technical-writing.label: Technical Writing & Documentation
technical-writing.description: Documentation of complex systems including data models, governance practices, and platform capabilities.

ai-native-expectation.label: AI fluency expected
ai-native-expectation.description: JD states comfort with AI tooling as a requirement or strong preference.

enabler-not-bottleneck.label: Enabler, not bottleneck
enabler-not-bottleneck.description: JD states the role should build systems that let others write well rather than being the sole writer.

governance-as-value-prop.label: Governance emphasized
governance-as-value-prop.description: JD frames governance, quality standards, or change management as a primary responsibility.

llm-first-process.label: LLM-first workflow
llm-first-process.description: JD prescribes starting work in an LLM as an explicit process step.

title-dilution.label: Title dilution
title-dilution.description: JD uses 'content systems' language for work that is primarily editorial production or content marketing.

title-responsibility-gap.label: Title ≠ stated responsibilities
title-responsibility-gap.description: The job title describes a narrower scope than the stated responsibilities cover.

model-behavior-design.label: Model behavior design
model-behavior-design.description: JD includes designing how an AI model responds conversationally as a stated responsibility.

language-infrastructure.label: Language as infrastructure
language-infrastructure.description: JD explicitly uses 'infrastructure' framing for language or content work.

process-signal.label: Process signal
process-signal.description: Role is not a content position but its stated process expectations are relevant to content disciplines.

content-adjacent.label: Content-adjacent
content-adjacent.description: Role applies content skills (taxonomy, governance, documentation) in a non-content-design context.

hybrid-role.label: Hybrid role
hybrid-role.description: JD combines content responsibilities with QA, engineering, or other disciplines in a single role.

classification-for-ml.label: Classification for ML
classification-for-ml.description: JD states taxonomy or classification work applied to machine learning training data.

content-marketing-adjacent.label: Content marketing adjacent
content-marketing-adjacent.description: JD uses systems or architecture language for work that is primarily content marketing.

geo-seo.label: GEO & SEO
geo-seo.description: JD states content architecture responsibilities for search and generative engine discovery.

structured-data.label: Structured data
structured-data.description: JD states Schema.org, JSON-LD, or entity modeling as a responsibility.
